facts.context: Which of the following organisms would most likely get its food energy only from consumers?
Changes at the molecular level can turn proto-oncogenes into oncogenes. These changes cause unregulated cell division. Which disease is caused by this transformation?
Which structure controls what enters both plant cells and animal cells?
A student compares a plant cell to an animal cell. Which structure will the student find ONLY in the plant cell?
Which series correctly represents the levels of organization in an organism?
A lumber company plants new trees where a forest once stood. This is an example of
Some pea plants always have peas with smooth skin. Other pea plants always have peas with wrinkled skin. How can a student best predict which kind of pea will grow from a seed that is planted?
facts.truth: by checking the type of peas growing on the parent plants
distractors: by checking the amount of water in the soil
by checking how often the pea plant receives sunlight
by checking how much space the plant will have to grow

facts.context: How do plants affect their environment?
facts.truth: Plants make food used by other organisms.

facts.context: Use the information below to answer the question. The human body is composed of different systems made of specialized cells, tissues, and organs. Each of these structures has a specific function that aids in the survival of the human species. What is the primary function of the excretory system?

Pond ecosystems depend on the water cycle to ensure the survival of the animals that live in or near the pond. If a drought reduces the water level, which population of animals would be most affected?
Some plants rely on hummingbirds for pollination. If a deadly disease infected the hummingbird population, what would most likely happen to the plants?
distractors: They would grow at a slower rate.

facts.context: Leaf cells are one type of tree cell. Which process occurs in a growing leaf cell?
A group of cells performing the same function in the body is called
All living organisms have certain needs in order to stay alive. Which is most necessary for both plants and animals?
Plant leaves have tiny pores. These pores help plants release water from their tissues into the
Which part of a plant makes sugar from carbon dioxide?
One function of the circulatory system is to carry cellular nutrients to the body. Which part of the circulatory system is responsible for exchanging nutrients and wastes?
Some animals have advantages and disadvantages because they are different. Which of the following has a disadvantage compared to other animals in its population?
facts.truth: a polar bear that has dark-colored fur
distractors: a rabbit that can run fast
a hummingbird with a long beak

facts.context: Which is a characteristic of both plants and animals?
Which cellular structure do organisms in the kingdoms Eubacteria, Archaebacteria, and Protista have in common?

In a particular habitat, Animal X has two sources of food. One source is found in the water and the other source is found on land. If the main predator of Animal X is found only on land, what is the most likely structural adaptation that will be selected for in the population of Animal X?
Which gas is given off by a plant during photosynthesis?
Which of the following processes makes it possible for plants to use energy from sunlight to produce their own food?
Which structure do a rabbit cell and a bacillus cell have in common?

By inserting a gene into crop plants, scientists have developed plants that are resistant to insects. If an insect eats the plant, the insect dies. Which practice is unnecessary with this new plant variety?

At concentrations of 0.1-1 µg/ml colchicine, a natural plant product, can cause the mitotic arrest of dividing cells at metaphase through disrupting the spindle apparatus. Colchicine interferes with which of these cell functions during cell division?
Most species of ducks have webbed feet. The webbed feet are an example of
When the environment changes more quickly than a species can adapt, the species may become
Why would animals most likely have to leave an area during a drought?
Which type of organism converts wastes and dead materials into nutrients that can be used by plants?

Biochemical reactions that require high temperatures to proceed are able to take place at body temperature within the cells of living organisms. Which factor is mainly responsible for allowing biochemical reactions to proceed at body temperature?
facts.truth: The cellular enzymes act as catalysts.
distractors: The small size of cells keeps reactants together.
The pH of the cytoplasm increases reaction rate.
The reactants are present in small quantities in the cell.

facts.context: Drake has started a garden. What can Drake do to enrich the soil so that his plants will grow better?
facts.truth: mix decomposed plant matter into the soil
distractors: add more water to the soil
increase the amount of pesticide he uses

facts.context: One example of a trait passed from a parent plant to its offspring is ___.
facts.truth: the color of the flowers on the plant
distractors: the amount of water in the plant's environment
the amount of sunlight shining on the plant
the nutrients in the soil where the plant grows

facts.context: Many organisms have structures for protection and survival. Which structure is most similar to tree bark?
Humans are protected from some infections by specialized cells which produce chemicals that
A scientist observed that the loss of plants near a stream resulted in more soil being washed downstream. How will the excess soil that travels downstream reduce biodiversity?
facts.truth: It will destroy the fish habitat.
distractors: It will provide nutrients to algae.
It will change the water temperature.
It will introduce an invasive species.

facts.context: When microorganisms deplete the oxygen in soil through respiration, the soil becomes

The Russian thistle, commonly known as the tumbleweed, is a big, round plant with many branches and short, stiff leaves. This plant spreads a large number of seeds when it breaks off from its stalk. The thistle is most likely found in what kind of environment?
A student wrote the following claim on a homework assignment: "Eukaryotic cells need more lipids than prokaryotic cells." Which statement below provides the BEST support for her claim?
distractors: Eukaryotic cells make up multicellular organisms.

facts.context: A black bear eats many different things, including grasses, roots, and berries. It also eats insects, fish, and other small animals. Which of these BEST describes a black bear?
From simplest to most complicated, how are multicellular organisms organized?
As some organisms die, their remains become buried by mud and are eventually compressed into a type of rock. Which of these is a rock type that forms from this process?
Animals depend on plants for many things, including food. What do plants depend on from animals?

A student prepared the following list of characteristics about a cellular organelle. present in animal cells present in plant cells helps make energy available to the cell Which of the following cellular structures is the student describing?
Brian knows deserts get less rain than other ecosystems. However, he also knows that plants can survive in the desert. Which characteristic of a plant most likely helps it live in a desert?
distractors: the ability to drop its leaves

facts.context: In an ecosystem, not all of the energy that is available is used by the organisms. Some energy becomes unavailable before it can be transferred to organisms in the next trophic level. How does the energy most likely become unavailable?
facts.truth: The energy is dissipated as heat.
distractors: The energy is stored as food.
The energy is trapped in dead organisms.
The energy is moved to another ecosystem.

facts.context: On a field trip in a wooded area, you see a small, strange object. You wonder whether is is a live animal. The best way to find out is to observe the object to see if it
distractors: can make a noise and has a lifelike color.

facts.context: How is xylem similar to phloem in a plant?
facts.truth: They both transport materials through the plant.
distractors: They both take in water from the soil.
They both make sugar in the leaves.
They both store waste products in the plant cells.

facts.context: Animals and plants are made up of a number of different chemical elements. What happens to all of these elements when animals and plants die?
facts.truth: They are recycled back into the environment.
distractors: They die with the animal or plant.

facts.context: A cell completes the synthesization of a protein by adding a carbohydrate to the protein's structure. At which site in the cell is the carbohydrate added?
Henry learned that without the right kind of energy, plants cannot survive. What form of energy is used by plants to perform photosynthesis?
As a corn plant ages, its leaves grow. Which process is directly responsible for the growth of these leaves?
facts.truth: the division of cells in the plant
distractors: the release of oxygen from the plant
the absorption of carbon dioxide in the stem
the transportation of nutrients from the roots

facts.context: Which body system typically recognizes, attacks, and destroys foreign cells or substances that may cause disease?
Cell membranes allow some molecules to move freely across the membrane, while other molecules are restricted. Which term best describes this capability of a cell membrane?
Which is the most cooperative relationship between species?
In which situation would osmosis most likely occur in cells?
facts.truth: across a permeable membrane that separates solutions of different concentrations
distractors: across a permeable membrane that separates solutions of the same concentration
across a nonpermeable membrane that separates solutions of the same concentration
across a nonpermeable membrane that separates solutions of different concentrations

facts.context: Needles on a cactus plant are actually leaves. This needle shape helps the plant survive by

Organisms contribute to the water cycle mainly through the process of
Which statement below best explains why several fossils of sea organisms were discovered buried deep in an area of dry land?
distractors: The area had a shift in climate.
A glacier once covered the area.

facts.context: Last year Steve was shorter than his brother. This year Steve is taller than his brother. What cell process is directly responsible for Steve's growth?

Over a long period, some flowering plants have become dependent on honey bees for pollination. In recent years, a form of mite has infected and killed the honey bees in many areas of the country. Since this has happened, the once bee-dependent plants with the best chance of surviving are those that
facts.truth: have always attracted many other insects as wells as honey bees.
distractors: grow taller so that the few remaining honey bees will find them.
change their flowers so that wind will fertilize them.
have seeds that stay alive for a long time in the environment.

facts.context: Plants growing in a pond release a gas that fish need to survive. Which gas do the plants release that fish use?

Scientists studying migrating birds and homing pigeons have discovered that they navigate by monitoring differences in the intensity and inclination of Earth's magnetic field. Special cells in bird beaks can detect these changes as the birds fly over Earth's surface. Which metal did the scientists find in the cells that birds use as a magnetometer?
A scientist found a fossil of an organism that had gills. When the organism was living, the area in which it lived was most likely covered by ___.
The wind helps many plants reproduce by ___.

Marta and Roger are comparing how long it takes two different types of seeds to sprout. They plant both types of seeds in similar pots, place them in a sunny window, and water them daily. If they want to conduct their investigation correctly, which information is most important to record?
facts.truth: the number of days until the seeds sprout
distractors: the size of pot used for growing the seeds

facts.context: During a biochemical reaction, pyruvate (C3H4O3) is converted into carbon dioxide (CO2) and water (H2O). In what cellular organelle does this biochemical reaction occur?

In 1989, an oil spill off the coast of Alaska led to a decline in the population of many organisms. Which other population was most likely affected by the drop in population of the fish, otters, and seals?
With the development of the microscope, scientists were able to see cells for the first time. Why was it difficult to view cells without a microscope?

Fertilizers can enable farmers to grow the same crop in a field for several years in a row. Farmers who use less fertilizer often rotate their crops by planting the crop one year and legumes, such as beans and clover, the following year. Fertilizer use and crop rotation with legumes both increase the availability of which of the following nutrients in soil?
Which two processes occur during the life spans of all animals?
Which of these cell parts store genetic instructions for the entire organism and transfers them from one generation to the next?
A plant that has traits identical to the parent plant provides evidence that the plant
Evidence of organisms that lived on Earth a long time ago would most likely be found in

While hiking through Granville State Forest, a student finds an unusual plant-like organism that appears to lack chlorophyll. When the student examines a sample using a microscope, he sees many cells with cell walls and no chloroplasts. This organism is most likely a member of what Kingdom?
Soil will pack down over time which decreases the ability of the soil to hold water. Which animals would best help the soil hold water?
facts.truth: earthworms making tunnels under the surface
distractors: cattle making depressions with their hooves
horses making trenches when they gallop
sheep pulling up plants by their roots

facts.context: Carbon dioxide produced by cells is removed from the body primarily by which body system?

During cellular respiration glucose molecules undergo a series of reactions. This process results in the formation of up to 38 molecules of high-energy ATP per each molecule of glucose. Which is the source of the energy stored in these ATP molecules?
facts.truth: chemical energy originally stored in the glucose molecule
distractors: kinetic energy generated by the chemical reactions
electric current created during the destruction of electrons in the glucose
potential energy from the difference in the concentration gradients of the cell

facts.context: A group of scientists is studying the effect of cell phone signals on honeybees. Before starting the investigation, the scientists stated that the bees exposed to cell phone signals will have difficulty finding their way back to the hive. Which term best describes the statement made by the group of scientists?

Five hundred flies of one species were sprayed with a new insecticide. Twenty-four hours later, nearly all the flies were dead. However, a few survived. Which principle best explains why some flies survived?
facts.truth: There is variation among individuals within a species.

facts.context: A student investigated the effect of the color of light on the growth of plants. Red, yellow, and green lights were used in the experiment. If another student were to conduct the same experiment, which would be the most important procedure to follow to yield the same results?
facts.truth: to use the same colors of lights
distractors: to do the investigation at the same time of year
to use the same chart for recording growth
to do the investigation in the same room

facts.context: Which of the following correctly lists the organizational hierarchy of organisms from simplest to most complex?
facts.truth: cells, tissues, organs, organ systems, organisms
distractors: cells, organs, tissues, organ systems, organisms
tissues, cells, organs, organ systems, organisms
tissues, organs, cells, organ systems, organisms

In Type 1 diabetes, the immune system attacks the pancreatic cells that produce insulin. Without insulin, people that have Type 1 diabetes are unable to regulate blood glucose levels. What two symptoms are most likely associated with Type 1 diabetes?
distractors: severe loss of hearing and vision
excessive loss of coordination and memory

facts.context: A girl found the skull of an animal. She did not know what the animal was but she was sure that it preyed on other animals for its food. What clue led to this conclusion?
facts.truth: Four of the teeth were long and pointed.
distractors: The skull was much longer than it was wide.
There was a projecting ridge along the top of the skull.
The jaws could move sideways as well as up and down.

facts.context: Which one of these animals does NOT lay eggs?

The element nitrogen can be found in simple, nonliving substances, such as air, and in complex molecules in living organisms, such as DNA. Which statement explains why nitrogen can exist in many compounds?
facts.truth: Nitrogen atoms can bond to different atoms.
distractors: Nitrogen atoms have different amounts of energy.

facts.context: Which form of energy do plants need to capture in order to perform photosynthesis?
A large number of species on Earth became extinct during a short time period about 65 million years ago. Based on this pattern of extinction, which is the most likely cause?
Which of the following is the most basic need for a plant?

distractors: help the plant to get moisture.
anchor the plant in the ground.

Cellulose is composed of many sugar molecules bonded together into long chains. Each sugar molecule contains carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen atoms. When cotton fabric is washed, wrinkles often form. The clothing industry uses chemicals to manufacture some cotton fabrics that are wrinkle-free. Dyes are also added to color the cellulose fibers in cotton. A feature that is common to both cellulose and sugar is that both are
Which animal is an example of an annelid?

Indigo buntings are birds that nest in Florida during the summer. Male buntings are bright blue in color while females are a dull brown. How does the dull brown color of the female buntings help the species survive?
facts.truth: It helps hide the birds while they sit on their nests.
distractors: It helps male birds find a mate.
It helps the birds find food more easily.
It helps the birds find the flock when migrating.
